The development of large language models has raised profound questions about the nature of intelligence, consciousness, and what it means to understand language. The global financial system is built on a complex web of institutions, regulations, and instruments that evolved over centuries of economic crises and innovations. Urban planning in the twenty-first century faces an increasingly complex set of challenges as cities continue to grow and grapple with issues of affordability, sustainability, mobility, and social equity. The philosophy of science investigates the foundations, methods, and implications of scientific inquiry. Questions about what distinguishes science from pseudoscience, how theories are confirmed or refuted by evidence, and whether scientific knowledge provides a true picture of reality have occupied philosophers for centuries. Karl Popper famously argued that falsifiability is the hallmark of genuine science, while Thomas Kuhn emphasized the role of paradigm shifts and the social dimensions of scientific communities.
